steps.title: Separate the water statements
steps.content: The first sentence must end after “the water turns to vapor.” The next sentence begins with the introductory phrase “With a pressure cooker pot, though,” before explaining the airtight seal.

steps.title: Reject faulty punctuation
steps.content: Choice B creates an ungrammatical connection with “and with.” Choice C uses a comma where a full sentence boundary is needed. Choice D fuses the ideas with no punctuation.

steps.title: Start a new sentence
steps.content: The remaining choice completes the first sentence with “vapor” and starts the next sentence with “With,” creating the needed boundary. The answer is A.
